name: Xiuhtecuhtli
description: Xiuhtecuhtli, also known as Huehueteotl, is a prominent Aztec deity representing fire, warmth, light, and time. He is considered one of the oldest and most revered gods in the Aztec pantheon, often depicted as an old man with a wrinkled face, signifying his ancient origins. Xiuhtecuhtli is the lord of fire, both in its destructive and life-giving aspects. He embodies the hearth fire, representing the center of the home and the family. As such, he is a symbol of domesticity, stability, and the cyclical nature of life. He is also associated with volcanoes, the sun, and the day count. Xiuhtecuhtli is often portrayed wearing a turquoise mosaic crown and carrying a censer, reflecting his royal status and his role in rituals. He is also associated with the number 17. He is a crucial figure in Aztec ceremonies, where fire played a central role in offerings and purification rites. His worship was essential for ensuring the prosperity and well-being of the community. Sacrifices and offerings were made to him to appease his power and maintain the cosmic balance. As Huehueteotl, the 'Old God,' he connects the present with the ancestral past, embodying the wisdom and continuity of tradition. He is also associated with the creation myth, believed to have been present at the beginning of time. His dual nature as both an ancient and a powerful force underscores his complex and significant role in Aztec religious beliefs. In some depictions, Xiuhtecuhtli is shown within a brazier, further emphasizing his connection to the life-sustaining flame.
subtitle: God of Fire, Hearth, and Time

keyMyths.title: The Old God
keyMyths.description: Xiuhtecuhtli, as Huehueteotl, is one of the oldest gods, present since the creation of the world, embodying ancient wisdom and tradition.

keyMyths.title: Lord of Fire
keyMyths.description: As the god of fire, Xiuhtecuhtli is central to many Aztec rituals, where fire is used for purification and offerings to the gods.

keyMyths.title: The Hearth Fire
keyMyths.description: Xiuhtecuhtli embodies the hearth fire, representing the center of the home, family unity, and domestic stability in Aztec society.
